pue_source: CyrusOne press release: PUE of 1.18 at 54 MW Frankfurt campus developed with Airedale
source: CyrusOne official facility page and datasheet
notes: Closed-loop water cooling system. First 9 MW phase delivered Q3 2024. Two three-story buildings each with dedicated 9 MW data halls. BREEAM Very Good, ISO 14001, ISO 50001 target certifications.

source: Equinix facility page; PeeringDB
notes: 114,097 sq ft retrofit facility in central Frankfurt. 11 MW power. Cold corridor / hot aisle design. Equinix global average PUE was 1.39 in 2024; per-IBX PUE not publicly disclosed.

source: Equinix IBX tech spec sheet (July 2021); datacentermap.com
notes: 25 MW power capacity, 592,015 sq ft total space, 244,394 sq ft colocation space. Equinix global average PUE 1.39 in 2024; per-IBX figure not disclosed.

source: Baxtel facility profile; Digital Realty Amsterdam page
notes: 11-story building, 5,000 sq m data space, 9 MW power. Uses outside air and groundwater for cooling; stores warm water underground (aquifer thermal energy storage). 150 kV dedicated substation. Tier III Uptime Institute certified.

source: NorthC official Aalsmeer page; DatacenterDynamics 2025
notes: 14 MW IT capacity, 4,600 sqm white space. Supports liquid cooling and immersion cooling for AI/HPC hardware. 4.5 MW expansion broke ground February 2025. Supplies waste heat to local school, daycare, swimming pool. ISO 14001, ISO 27001, ISO 9001, PCI DSS certified.

source: Digital Realty Amsterdam expansion press release; baxtel.com
notes: After three-phase completion: 5,500 sqm, 7 MW total capacity. Third phase added 2,048 sqm and 2.4 MW. Carrier-neutral colocation.

source: Equinix press release completing BT Ireland acquisition; newsroom.equinix.com
notes: Acquired from BT for EUR 59M in November 2025. Carrier-neutral, purpose-built facility comprising two independent data center buildings covering 120,000 sq ft (11,148 sqm). 10 MW capacity. Expansion headroom for AI workloads.

source: Equinix press release completing BT Ireland acquisition; newsroom.equinix.com
notes: Acquired from BT alongside Citywest (DB1). 3.5 MW, 40,000 sq ft facility on Dublin's north side. Expansion headroom for AI workloads.

pue_source: EcoDataCenter official facility page — PUE of 1.2 achieved according to ISO standards
source: EcoDataCenter official website; UNFCCC climate action database
notes: World's first data center built in wood. 80 MW total capacity across six buildings (each 8 MW, 3,400 sqm white space). Air, direct liquid cooling, and immersion cooling supported. 100% renewable electricity (75% hydropower, 25% wind). Heat recovery to Falun CHP plant. ISO 9001, ISO 14001, ISO 27001, EN 50600 certified. Operational since 2019.

pue_source: DigiPlex/STACK published PUE of 1.1 for Nordic portfolio using Air-to-Air cooling technology designed for Norway/Sweden climate
source: Dgtl Infra analysis of DigiPlex Nordic portfolio; DataCenterJournal
notes: Formerly DigiPlex Stockholm 1 (DS1). 10 MW, 6,000 sqm white space on 4-hectare campus. DigiPlex proprietary Air-to-Air free cooling technology — design PUE 1.1. Acquired by IPI Partners; operated as STACK EMEA.

pue_source: Green Mountain official SVG-Rennesøy facility page — average PUE 1.18
source: Green Mountain official website; Victaulic project case study
notes: Fjord cooling: gravity-fed seawater pipes to 100m depth at constant 8C year-round; titanium heat exchanger closed loop. 25 MW capacity, 22,600 sqm across 6 two-story mountain halls. Built 2013. Tier III certified. N+N cooling redundancy. No water consumption for cooling.

pue_source: atNorth DEN01 facility page: PUE ≤1.2 design target; first phase live Q4 2025
source: atNorth official facility page
notes: 30 MW total capacity, 27,728 sqm campus. DLC-ready supporting 100+ kW liquid-cooled racks. Average rack density 40 kW. Chilled water with mechanical cooling and free-cooling capabilities N+1. District heat reuse. First phase operational Q4 2025. HPC and AI primary workload.

pue_source: OVHcloud states portfolio-level PUE range 1.1–1.2; SBG5-specific figure not separated
source: OVHcloud official SBG5 press release
notes: Inaugurated September 12, 2022. EUR 30M investment. 1,700 sqm, 19 isolated rooms with masonry compartmentalization. Water-cooled (closed-circuit dry coolers, no CRAC units). WUE below 0.2 l/kWh. OVHcloud proprietary water cooling since 2003. First facility of hyper-resilience plan.

source: DatacenterDynamics; Data4 press release partnership with OVHcloud January 2024
notes: First liquid cooling deployment at Marcoussis: OVHcloud DLC integrated into Data4 cold water loops. ~25% electricity savings vs air cooling. WUE 0.06 l/kWh IT. Data4 operates three Paris campuses totalling 375 MW combined; Marcoussis is primary campus. 250 MW DLC design projects across all Data4 DCs in progress as of 2024.

pue_source: CoreWeave/MERLIN Edged official inauguration announcement — design PUE 1.15
source: CoreWeave investor press release; Baxtel; MERLIN Edged official announcement
notes: Inaugurated May 13 2025 by Mayor Jaume Collboni. Hosts 10,224 NVIDIA H200 GPUs with InfiniBand. 15 MW initial phase, 22 MW total capacity. PUE 1.15 (vs European average 1.46). Zero water for cooling; 100% renewables. Liquid cooling mandatory from day one. Blackwell expansion planned 2026.

pue_source: Quetta Data Centers press release December 2025: PUE target less than 1.15
source: Azora / Quetta Data Centers press release
notes: Construction underway as of December 2025. 20 MW, closed-loop direct liquid cooling (DLC), WUE = 0, zero water consumption. PUE target <1.15. 100% renewables. Part of Quetta 6-DC Spain/Portugal plan, EUR 500M+ investment.

facility_name: Quetta Data Centers Barcelona (Molins de Rei)
pue_source: Quetta Data Centers press release December 2025: PUE target less than 1.15 across all Quetta facilities
source: Azora / Quetta Data Centers press release
notes: Construction underway as of December 2025. Madrid + Barcelona together reach 30 MW (50% of 60 MW Quetta target). DLC closed-loop, zero water, <1.15 PUE target. Individual MW for Barcelona site not separately disclosed.

pue_source: Submer/Telefonica case study: rack-level PUE below 1.10 recorded at Bellas Vistas deployment
source: Submer Technologies case study; Telefonica press room
notes: Submer SmartPod 50 kW immersion cooling pilot at Telefonica central office. Rack-level PUE below 1.10. Coolant stable below 55C under maximum load. Tier III Uptime compliant. Edge/5G use case. No evaporative water consumption. Biodegradable non-conductive dielectric fluid.

source: Atman official WAW-3 opening press release; atman.pl
notes: Opened September 18 2025. First phase: 14.4 MW IT power, 6,324 sqm data hall space. Full campus (3 buildings): 43 MW IT, 19,000 sqm, 50,000+ servers, 36 data halls. 5.5-hectare site 30 min from Warsaw center. Closed-loop liquid cooling. AI/hyperscale workloads. 100% renewable energy.

address: Via Reggio Emilia 39, 20054 Segrate, Milan, Italy
pue_source: CyrusOne press release: low PUE via closed-loop cooling and optimum chilled water temperatures to maximize free cooling hours
source: CyrusOne official Milan MIL1 press release; DatacenterDynamics
notes: Municipality of Segrate, East Milan. 27 MW IT capacity, 9,000 sqm technical space, one three-story building with three 9 MW data halls. Delivered in three phases. Operational Q3 2027 (first phase). BREEAM Very Good minimum target. 100% renewable energy. Solar panels for ancillary areas.

pue_source: CyrusOne MIL2 uses same closed-loop cooling solution as MIL1 per operator documentation
source: CyrusOne official MIL2 page; DatacenterDynamics
notes: Second CyrusOne Milan facility. 54 MW IT capacity, 18,000 sqm technical space, two three-story buildings. Free cooling when possible via closed-loop solution. Planned alongside MIL1.

address: Frankfurt Westside, Frankfurt am Main, Germany
pue_source: CyrusOne FRA7 press release: low PUE via closed-loop water cooling system and sustainable specifications
source: CyrusOne press release; DatacenterDynamics groundbreaking report
notes: 63,000 sqm plot at Frankfurt Westside regeneration project. First 9 MW phase construction Q1 2026, target Q2 2026 completion. Full campus: 63 MW planned. E.ON partnership for 61 MW on-site power and cooling plant (June 2025). Closed-loop water cooling.

address: Avenida del Maresme 43-45, Sant Adrià de Besòs, Barcelona, Spain
source: Digital Realty BCN1 facility page; DatacenterHawk; Barcelona Catalonia economic development
notes: EUR 230M investment. 15,000 sqm site in Sant Adrià de Besòs. 15 MW IT capacity. 300m from Cable Landing Station; only global carrier-neutral facility in Barcelona region. Expected to commission early 2026. Digital Realty earmarked EUR 500M+ for Madrid and Barcelona combined.

pue_source: OVHcloud states portfolio PUE range 1.1–1.2 with Roubaix as primary campus; Smart Rack cooling deployment in Roubaix announced 2024
source: OVHcloud newsroom — Smart Datacenter announcement; OVHcloud global infrastructure page
notes: OVHcloud primary campus. New OVHcloud Smart Racks deployed in Roubaix room with ~60 racks and 2,000 servers with new proprietary cooling system. Water cooling R&D at scale since 2003. Portfolio PUE 1.1–1.2. HVO diesel pilot also underway in Roubaix metro (alongside Frankfurt and Paris).

source: NorthC press release on European expansion into Germany
notes: NorthC announced new data center locations in Frankfurt and Berlin as part of international growth strategy. No MW or address details disclosed. NorthC supports liquid and immersion cooling across its portfolio per company marketing materials.

pue_source: EcoDataCenter design standard PUE 1.2 (ISO certified) applied to expansion; EUR 600M financing secured September 2025
source: DataCentre Magazine — EcoDataCenter Vertiv expansion announcement; IT Pro
notes: EUR 600M debt financing secured September 2025. Planned capacity 240–360 MW, making it Sweden's largest DC. ~20km from EcoDataCenter 1. Air, DLC, and immersion cooling supported (same model as EDC1). Target 2027 opening. Vertiv selected for chilled water cooling units.

source: Penta Infra Copenhagen data center page
notes: 7.6 MW planned IT capacity. Campus model with CPH02 and CPH03 expansion phases planned. Smaller edge-type facility targeting Copenhagen metro.

source: Switch Datacenters press release July 2025; Dutch Data Center Association
notes: Land and permits secured July 2025. 90 MW power capacity (60 MW IT load first phase), modular design to 100+ MW IT. Supports air-cooled and liquid-cooled systems. Hybrid dry cooling technology. Hyperscale and AI target workloads.
